title: Iliamna corei
taxonomy: Iliamna corei is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. It is native to the western United States, specifically in the states of California, Oregon, and Washington. The plant is classified as a perennial that grows in wetland habitats, often near water or in areas with high moisture levels. Iliamna corei is a member of the genus Iliamna, which consists of several species of flowering plants native to North America. The classification of Iliamna corei is further supported by its distinctive characteristics, such as its large, showy flowers and hairy stems.

intro: Iliamna corei, also known as Core's mountain hollyhock, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to promote cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation due to its potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Iliamna corei include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Iliamna corei is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to support cardiovascular health and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress.
